agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver density bonus and inclusionary zoning updates for Bill 16 compliance
agenda_briefs.summary: Council has adopted amendments to Vancouver's density bonus provisions and inclusionary zoning rules to comply with provincial Bill 16. Key changes include: deleting the Heritage Amenity Shares purchase option from the Transfer of Density policy; replacing references to 'affordable housing shares' and 'amenity shares' with 'inclusionary zoning' in the Community Amenity Contributions Policy; increasing the hotel bonus exclusion for receiver sites from 15% to 20%; and renaming 'Calculation of Density' to 'Calculation of Floor Space' in the Downtown District and IC-3 District policies. These amendments take effect on July 14, 2026.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Review your development project's density bonus and inclusionary zoning obligations under the new rules effective July 14, 2026.

key_dates.label: Vancouver density bonus and inclusionary zoning amendments take effect

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver council to vote on vacancy tax exemption for new inventory
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the Vacancy Tax By-law No. 11674. The change would create an exemption for vacant new inventory — meaning newly built or newly completed residential units that are not yet sold or occupied. If approved, this would allow owners of new, unsold units to avoid paying the vacancy tax for a set period. The proposal is not yet law; it is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this exemption could affect how you report new units on your vacancy tax return.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: New consultation rules for Vancouver rezoning applications
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed bylaw that would require developers to consult with the public before applying to rezone land in Vancouver. A rezoning is a request to change what a piece of land can be used for (for example, from single-family homes to apartments). If approved, this new rule would apply to any rezoning application that asks to change the city's Official Development Plan — the city's long-term plan for growth. The goal is to give neighbours and businesses a chance to have their say early in the process, before a formal application is submitted. This is not yet law; it is up for debate and a vote at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments if you own property or a business that may be rezoned.

agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a below-market ground lease and a transition grant to Marpole Terrace Co-op for the property at 8695 Southwest Marine Drive. The lease would run until September 30, 2066, with an option to extend 20 years. Rent would be calculated using the Co-op Lease Methodology, with an estimated Year 1 annual land rent of $241,065 ($515.10 per unit per month). A 10-year transition grant would ease the rent impact. The co-op has 39 units; 15 are Rent-Geared-to-Income (RGI) and 24 are non-RGI. The recommendation requires at least 8 affirmative votes (two-thirds of Council) under Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver updates development cost rules for 2027–2036
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote July 14, 2026 on a new Amenity Cost Charge (ACC) bylaw and amendments to DCL, UDCL, and ASDCL bylaws, effective September 30, 2026. ACC rates are phased in for residential projects over 1.5 FSR (50% reduced rate Sept 30, 2026; full rate Sept 30, 2027). Annual inflationary adjustments of 3.0% apply Sept 30, 2027–2029. The False Creek Flats DCL is removed (rates to $0/sq ft). DCL/UDCL area expands to Central Waterfront Port Lands. Public Art Policy for rezoned developments ends for applications submitted after Sept 30, 2026.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Review proposed ACC rates in Appendix A and DCL allocation changes; prepare for Sept 30, 2026 effective date if approved.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider proposed 2027-2030 Capital Plan for Vancouver
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council will vote on July 14, 2026 on a proposed $3.5 billion 2027-2030 Capital Plan. The plan includes $790 million in borrowing subject to a plebiscite at the October civic election, with questions for Transportation ($292M), Community Facilities ($438M), and Parks ($60M). Key investments include $104M for street/sidewalk rehabilitation and $400M for renewal of five community centres. A 2% infrastructure levy is already approved.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Contact your city councillor before the July 14 council meeting to provide feedback on the proposed capital plan and borrowing questions.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Vancouver General Hospital campus policy update under Broadway Plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a policy update for the Vancouver General Hospital (VGH) campus that would repeal the 2000 Policy Statement and 2006 Public Realm Plan, and add a new Policy Appendix to the Broadway Plan. The update sets guiding principles for land use, density, height, built form, public open space, heritage, transportation, sustainability, public benefits, and development phasing over 30+ years. It would replace the current 2.6 FSR limit and guide rezoning by Vancouver Coastal Health Authority.
